back_translation: From the blue and the purple and the scarlet yarn they made garments of service to minister in the sanctuary; and they made the holy garments that were for Aaron, just as the LORD commanded Moses.
He made the ephod — of gold, blue and purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen.
They hammered out thin sheets of gold and cut threads to interweave with the blue, the purple, and the scarlet yarn and the fine linen — the work of a skilled craftsman.
Shoulder pieces they made for it, joining at its two ends — it was joined.

And the skillfully woven waistband of the ephod that was on it was of one piece with it, of the same workmanship — gold, blue and purple, and scarlet yarn, and fine twisted linen — just as the LORD commanded Moses.
They mounted the onyx stones in gold filigree settings, engraved like seal engravings with the names of the sons of Israel.
He set them on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as memorial stones for the sons of Israel, just as the LORD commanded Moses.
He made the breastpiece — the work of a skilled craftsman, like the work of the ephod — gold, blue and purple, and scarlet yarn, and fine twisted linen.
Square it was, double they made the breastpiece — a span its length and a span its width, doubled.
They filled it with four rows of stones — a row of ruby, topaz, and emerald — the first row.
And the second row — turquoise, sapphire, and diamond.
And the third row — jacinth, agate, and amethyst.
And the fourth row — beryl, onyx, and jasper — mounted in gold filigree settings in their mountings.
The stones were according to the names of the sons of Israel — twelve, according to their names — like seal engravings, each with his name, for the twelve tribes.
They made on the breastpiece twisted chains, the work of cord — pure gold.
They made two gold filigree settings and two gold rings, and put the two rings on the two corners of the breastpiece.
They put the two gold cords on the two rings at the corners of the breastpiece.
The two ends of the two cords they put on the two filigree settings, and attached them to the shoulder pieces of the ephod on its front.
They made two gold rings and set them on the two corners of the breastpiece, on its edge that was toward the inside of the ephod.
They made two gold rings and put them on the two shoulder pieces of the ephod below, on its front, close to its seam, above the woven waistband of the ephod.

They tied the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a cord of blue, so that it would be on the waistband of the ephod and the breastpiece would not come loose from the ephod, just as the LORD commanded Moses.
He made the robe of the ephod, the work of a weaver, entirely of blue.
And the opening of the robe in the midst of it was like the opening of a coat of mail, a binding for its opening round about — it would not be torn.
They made on the hem of the robe pomegranates of blue and purple and scarlet yarn — twisted.
They made bells of pure gold and put the bells in the midst of the pomegranates on the hem of the robe round about, in the midst of the pomegranates.
A bell and a pomegranate, a bell and a pomegranate, on the hem of the robe round about — for ministering, just as the LORD commanded Moses.
They made the tunics of fine linen, the work of a weaver, for Aaron and for his sons.
And the turban of fine linen, and the headbands of fine linen, and the linen undergarments of fine twisted linen.
And the sash of fine twisted linen and blue and purple and scarlet yarn — the work of an embroiderer — just as the LORD commanded Moses.
They made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold, and they wrote on it an inscription like seal engravings: HOLY TO THE LORD.
They put on it a cord of blue, to mount it on the turban above, just as the LORD commanded Moses.
All the work of the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting was finished. The sons of Israel did according to all that the LORD commanded Moses — so they did.
They brought the tabernacle to Moses — the tent and all its vessels, its clasps, its frames, its crossbars, and its pillars and its bases.
And the covering of ram skins dyed red, the covering of fine leather, and the veil of the screen.
The ark of the Testimony and its poles and the mercy seat.
The table, all its vessels, and the Bread of the Presence.
The pure lampstand, its lamps — the lamps of the row — and all its vessels, and the oil for the light.
The golden altar, the anointing oil, the fragrant incense, and the screen for the entrance of the tent.
The bronze altar and its bronze grating that was for it, its poles, and all its vessels; the basin and its stand.

The hangings of the courtyard, its pillars and its bases, and the screen for the gate of the courtyard, its cords and its pegs, and all the vessels of the service of the tabernacle for the Tent of Meeting.
The woven garments for ministering in the sanctuary, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons to serve as priests.
According to all that the LORD commanded Moses, so the sons of Israel did all the work.
Moses saw all the work, and behold, they had done it — just as the LORD commanded, so they did. And Moses blessed them.
